1→# Slice: AddTask 2→ 3→## Pattern 4→ 5→**Command** (State Change) 6→ 7→## Summary 8→ 9→The user types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the 10→bottom of the active list with the given description and a default position. 11→ 12→## Diagram 13→ 14→```mermaid 15→flowchart LR 16→ UI["Active Task List
input field + Add button"] 17→ CMD["AddTask"]:::command 18→ EVT["TaskAdded"]:::event 19→ VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view 20→ 21→ UI -->|"description"| CMD 22→ CMD --> EVT 23→ EVT --> VIEW 24→ 25→ classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff 26→ classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff 27→ classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff 28→``` 29→ 30→## Command: AddTask 31→ 32→| Field | Type | Description | 33→|-------|------|-------------| 34→| description | text | What needs to be done (non-empty) | 35→ 36→**Triggered by:** User types in the input field and clicks [+ Add] or presses 37→Enter. 38→ 39→**Rejection conditions:** 40→- Description is empty or blank. 41→ 42→## Event: TaskAdded 43→ 44→| Field | Type | Description | 45→|-------|------|-------------| 46→| task_id | identifier | Unique identifier assigned to the new task | 47→| description | text | The task description provided by the user | 48→| position | number | Position in the list (bottom / lowest priority) | 49→ 50→## Wireframe 51→ 52→``` 53→+--------------------------------------------------+ 54→| My Tasks [Completed (0)] | 55→+--------------------------------------------------+ 56→| | 57→| [ Buy groceries_________________] [+ Add] | 58→| ^--- user types here | 59→| | 60→| No tasks yet. Add one above! | 61→| | 62→+--------------------------------------------------+ 63→ 64→After submitting: 65→ 66→+--------------------------------------------------+ 67→| My Tasks [Completed (0)] | 68→+--------------------------------------------------+ 69→| | 70→| [ What needs to be done? ] [+ Add] | 71→| | 72→| :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | 73→| | 74→+--------------------------------------------------+ 75→``` 76→ 77→## Read Model Impact 78→ 79→**ActiveTaskList:** New task appears at the bottom of the list. 80→ 81→## GWT Scenarios 82→ 83→### Scenario: Add task to an empty list 84→ 85→**Given** (prior events): 86→- (none -- no tasks exist yet) 87→ 88→**When** (command): 89→- AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" } 90→ 91→**Then** (events produced): 92→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } 93→ 94→--- 95→ 96→### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom) 97→ 98→**Given** (prior events): 99→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } 100→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } 101→ 102→**When** (command): 103→- AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" } 104→ 105→**Then** (events produced): 106→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 } 107→ 108→--- 109→ 110→### Scenario: Add task when some tasks have been completed (position based on active + completed count) 111→ 112→**Given** (prior events): 113→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } 114→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } 115→- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" } 116→ 117→**When** (command): 118→- AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" } 119→ 120→**Then** (events produced): 121→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-004", description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 } 122→ 123→**Note:** Empty/blank description is prevented by the type system (NonEmptyString domain type), not by command rejection. No GWT scenario is needed for that case. 124→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.content:
1→# Slice: ActiveTaskList
     2→
     3→## Pattern
     4→
     5→**View** (State View / Read Model)
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→A read model that projects all events into an ordered list of active
    10→(incomplete, non-deleted) tasks. This powers the main view of the application.
    11→
    12→## Diagram
    13→
    14→```mermaid
    15→flowchart LR
    16→    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    17→    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    18→    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    19→    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    20→    E5["TaskReprioritized"]:::event
    21→    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    22→    VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view
    23→
    24→    E1 --> VIEW
    25→    E2 --> VIEW
    26→    E3 --> VIEW
    27→    E4 --> VIEW
    28→    E5 --> VIEW
    29→    E6 --> VIEW
    30→
    31→    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    32→    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
    33→```
    34→
    35→## Read Model: ActiveTaskList
    36→
    37→### Fields
    38→
    39→| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
    40→|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
    41→| tasks | list | (all events) | Ordered list of active tasks |
    42→| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
    43→| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
    44→| tasks[].position | number | TaskAdded.position, TaskReprioritized.position, TaskReopened (top) | Current priority position |
    45→| completed_count | number | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1), TaskDeleted (if completed, -1) | Count of completed tasks for badge |
    46→
    47→### Filters
    48→
    49→- Only tasks where **completed = false** AND **deleted = false**.
    50→
    51→### Ordering
    52→
    53→- By position ascending. Position 1 = highest priority (top of list).
    54→
    55→### Event Processing Rules
    56→
    57→| Event | Effect on Read Model |
    58→|-------|---------------------|
    59→| TaskAdded | Add new task at the bottom of the list (highest position number) |
    60→| TaskCompleted | Remove task from active list. Increment completed_count. |
    61→| TaskReopened | Add task back to active list at position 1 (top). Shift existing tasks down. Decrement completed_count. |
    62→| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task. |
    63→| TaskReprioritized | Move task to new position. Shift other tasks to accommodate. |
    64→| TaskDeleted | Remove task from active list. If task was completed, decrement completed_count. |
    65→
    66→### Information Completeness Check
    67→
    68→| Display Element | Source |
    69→|----------------|--------|
    70→| Task description text | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
    71→| Task order | TaskAdded.position / TaskReprioritized.position / TaskReopened (top) |
    72→| Completed count badge | TaskCompleted / TaskReopened / TaskDeleted |
    73→| Empty state message | Derived: tasks list is empty |
    74→
    75→All fields trace to events. No missing data.
    76→
    77→## Wireframe
    78→
    79→```
    80→+--------------------------------------------------+
    81→|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
    82→+--------------------------------------------------+
    83→|                                                    |
    84→|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
    85→|                                                    |
    86→|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
    87→|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
    88→|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
    89→|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
    90→|                                                    |
    91→+--------------------------------------------------+
    92→```
    93→
    94→## GWT Scenarios
    95→
    96→### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to the bottom of an empty list
    97→
    98→**Given** (current projection state):
    99→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
   100→
   101→**When** (event to process):
   102→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
   103→
   104→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   105→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }
   106→
   107→---
   108→
   109→### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to the bottom of a populated list
   110→
   111→**Given** (current projection state):
   112→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }], completed_count: 0 }
   113→
   114→**When** (event to process):
   115→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }
   116→
   117→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   118→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }
   119→
   120→---
   121→
   122→### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task from active list and increments completed count
   123→
   124→**Given** (current projection state):
   125→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }], completed_count: 1 }
   126→
   127→**When** (event to process):
   128→- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
   129→
   130→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   131→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 2 }
   132→
   133→---
   134→
   135→### Scenario: TaskReopened adds task to top of list and decrements completed count
   136→
   137→**Given** (current projection state):
   138→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 2 }], completed_count: 2 }
   139→
   140→**When** (event to process):
   141→- TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
   142→
   143→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   144→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 1 }
   145→
   146→**Note:** The reopened task's description ("Call the dentist") must be resolved from prior events. Existing tasks shift down (positions increment by 1).
   147→
   148→---
   149→
   150→### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates description of an active task
   151→
   152→**Given** (current projection state):
   153→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }
   154→
   155→**When** (event to process):
   156→- TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
   157→
   158→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   159→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office", position: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }
   160→
   161→---
   162→
   163→### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for a completed task does not affect active list
   164→
   165→**Given** (current projection state):
   166→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }
   167→
   168→**When** (event to process):
   169→- TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist office" }
   170→
   171→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   172→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }
   173→
   174→---
   175→
   176→### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to a new position (higher priority)
   177→
   178→**Given** (current projection state):
   179→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }
   180→
   181→**When** (event to process):
   182→- TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
   183→
   184→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   185→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }
   186→
   187→---
   188→
   189→### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes an active task from the list
   190→
   191→**Given** (current projection state):
   192→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }], completed_count: 1 }
   193→
   194→**When** (event to process):
   195→- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
   196→
   197→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   198→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }
   199→
   200→---
   201→
   202→### Scenario: TaskDeleted for a completed task decrements completed count but does not change active list
   203→
   204→**Given** (current projection state):
   205→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 2 }
   206→
   207→**When** (event to process):
   208→- TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" }
   209→
   210→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   211→- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 1 }
   212→
   213→**Note:** The projection must track whether a deleted task was completed in order to correctly adjust the completed_count.
   214→

I need to design a detailed implementation plan for running 7 vertical slices in parallel using Claude Code Agent Teams + Git Worktrees. Here's the full context:

## Project Context

This is an event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list app using Commanded + Ecto projections. Infrastructure is complete (Commanded app, router, event store, repo all configured). No production code exists yet - only infrastructure modules.

## Architecture

- **Commanded-native aggregates** with aggregate-per-command pattern (via commanded_boilerplate)
- **Domain types** via newt library: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition
- **Context module** `TodoList.Tasks` as API boundary
- **Testing**: 3 layers - aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E
- **Quality gates**: compile (warnings-as-errors), format, dialyzer, tests

## The 7 Vertical Slices (all independent, all ready)

1. **AddTask + ActiveTaskList** (Command + View) - 3 GWT scenarios
2. **CompleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
3. **CompletedTaskList** (View) - 6 GWT scenarios
4. **ReopenTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
5. **ChangeTaskDescription** (Command) - 4 GWT scenarios
6. **ReprioritizeTask** (Command) - 3 GWT scenarios
7. **DeleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios

## GWT Scenarios Location
docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/ contains:
- add-task.md, complete-task.md, reopen-task.md, change-task-description.md
- reprioritize-task.md, delete-task.md, active-task-list.md, completed-task-list.md

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

The plan is to:
1. Create 7 git worktrees (one per slice)
2. Create an Agent Team with 7 teammates (one per slice)
3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

These files will be created/modified by ALL slices independently in their worktrees:
- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema
- `lib/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector.ex` - Event handlers
- `lib/todo_list/tasks.ex` - Context module API
- Domain types: `lib/todo_list/task_id.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_description.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_position.ex`
- Ecto migration(s) for the tasks table
- Router/LiveView routes

Each worktree will have its own copy, so no overwrites during development. Merge conflicts resolved during PR process.

## Files Unique Per Slice

- Command modules: `lib/todo_list/task/commands/{slice_name}.ex`
- Event modules: `lib/todo_list/task/events/{event_name}.ex`
- Test files (should be per-command to avoid conflicts):
  - `test/todo_list/task/commands/{command_name}_test.exs`
  - `test/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector_test.exs` (shared but in separate worktrees)

## SDLC Workflow Per Slice

Each teammate should follow:
1. Pick up their assigned dot task (`dot on <task-id>`)
2. Run TDD cycle for each GWT scenario:
   - RED: Write ONE failing test (one assertion)
   - DOMAIN: Review for type safety, create domain types
   - GREEN: Minimal implementation to pass
   - DOMAIN: Review implementation
3. After all scenarios pass, create PR (/sdlc:pr)

## Questions to Address in the Plan

1. **Worktree creation**: How to create 7 worktrees with proper branch names
2. **Team structure**: How to create the team and assign slices
3. **Teammate prompts**: What each teammate needs to know (GWT scenarios, file paths, architecture decisions)
4. **Dot task management**: Each worktree has its own .dots/ - how to handle this
5. **PR strategy**: Should all PRs target main? Order of merging?
6. **Merge conflict resolution**: Strategy for shared files
7. **Lead role**: What the team lead does while teammates work
8. **Shutdown and cleanup**: After all PRs merged

## Key Constraints

- Agent Teams are enabled with tmux mode
- Teammates are full Claude Code sessions with access to all SDLC skills and subagents
- "No nested teams" - teammates can use subagents but not create teams
- Each teammate loads CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills automatically
- Teammates don't inherit lead's conversation history - need full context in spawn prompt
- The lead should use delegate mode (coordination only, no direct code edits)

Please design a comprehensive, step-by-step plan covering all of the above. Be specific about:
- Exact git commands for worktree creation
- Team creation parameters
- Spawn prompts for each teammate (what context they need)
- Task dependency structure
- PR merge order strategy
- How the lead monitors and coordinates

content.content:
1→# Slice: CompletedTaskList
     2→
     3→## Pattern
     4→
     5→**View** (State View / Read Model)
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→A read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
    10→tasks. This powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
    11→and reopen or delete tasks.
    12→
    13→## Diagram
    14→
    15→```mermaid
    16→flowchart LR
    17→    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    18→    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    19→    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    20→    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    21→    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    22→    VIEW["CompletedTaskList"]:::view
    23→
    24→    E1 --> VIEW
    25→    E2 --> VIEW
    26→    E3 --> VIEW
    27→    E4 --> VIEW
    28→    E6 --> VIEW
    29→
    30→    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    31→    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Read Model: CompletedTaskList
    35→
    36→### Fields
    37→
    38→| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
    39→|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
    40→| tasks | list | (filtered events) | List of completed tasks |
    41→| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
    42→| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
    43→
    44→### Filters
    45→
    46→- Only tasks where **completed = true** AND **deleted = false**.
    47→
    48→### Ordering
    49→
    50→- By completion time descending (most recently completed first). Completion
    51→  time is derived from the TaskCompleted event timestamp.
    52→
    53→### Event Processing Rules
    54→
    55→| Event | Effect on Read Model |
    56→|-------|---------------------|
    57→| TaskAdded | No effect (new tasks are not completed). |
    58→| TaskCompleted | Add task to completed list. |
    59→| TaskReopened | Remove task from completed list. |
    60→| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task (if present in list). |
    61→| TaskDeleted | Remove task from completed list (if present). |
    62→
    63→### Information Completeness Check
    64→
    65→| Display Element | Source |
    66→|----------------|--------|
    67→| Task description text (with strikethrough) | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
    68→| Task ordering (most recent first) | TaskCompleted event timestamp |
    69→| Empty state | Derived: tasks list is empty |
    70→
    71→All fields trace to events. No missing data.
    72→
    73→## Wireframe
    74→
    75→```
    76→+--------------------------------------------------+
    77→|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |
    78→+--------------------------------------------------+
    79→|                                                    |
    80→|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    81→|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    82→|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    83→|                                                    |
    84→+--------------------------------------------------+
    85→```
    86→
    87→## GWT Scenarios
    88→
    89→### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
    90→
    91→**Given** (current projection state):
    92→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
    93→
    94→**When** (event to process):
    95→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
    96→
    97→**Then** (resulting projection state):
    98→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
    99→
   100→---
   101→
   102→### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
   103→
   104→**Given** (current projection state):
   105→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
   106→
   107→**When** (event to process):
   108→- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
   109→
   110→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   111→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }
   112→
   113→**Note:** The task's description must be resolved from prior events (TaskAdded or TaskDescriptionChanged).
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
   118→
   119→**Given** (current projection state):
   120→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }
   121→
   122→**When** (event to process):
   123→- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
   124→
   125→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   126→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

data.prompt:
I need to design a detailed implementation plan for running 7 vertical slices in parallel using Claude Code Agent Teams + Git Worktrees. Here's the full context:

## Project Context

This is an event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list app using Commanded + Ecto projections. Infrastructure is complete (Commanded app, router, event store, repo all configured). No production code exists yet - only infrastructure modules.

## Architecture

- **Commanded-native aggregates** with aggregate-per-command pattern (via commanded_boilerplate)
- **Domain types** via newt library: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition
- **Context module** `TodoList.Tasks` as API boundary
- **Testing**: 3 layers - aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E
- **Quality gates**: compile (warnings-as-errors), format, dialyzer, tests

## The 7 Vertical Slices (all independent, all ready)

1. **AddTask + ActiveTaskList** (Command + View) - 3 GWT scenarios
2. **CompleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
3. **CompletedTaskList** (View) - 6 GWT scenarios
4. **ReopenTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
5. **ChangeTaskDescription** (Command) - 4 GWT scenarios
6. **ReprioritizeTask** (Command) - 3 GWT scenarios
7. **DeleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios

## GWT Scenarios Location
docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/ contains:
- add-task.md, complete-task.md, reopen-task.md, change-task-description.md
- reprioritize-task.md, delete-task.md, active-task-list.md, completed-task-list.md

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

The plan is to:
1. Create 7 git worktrees (one per slice)
2. Create an Agent Team with 7 teammates (one per slice)
3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

These files will be created/modified by ALL slices independently in their worktrees:
- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema
- `lib/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector.ex` - Event handlers
- `lib/todo_list/tasks.ex` - Context module API
- Domain types: `lib/todo_list/task_id.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_description.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_position.ex`
- Ecto migration(s) for the tasks table
- Router/LiveView routes

Each worktree will have its own copy, so no overwrites during development. Merge conflicts resolved during PR process.

## Files Unique Per Slice

- Command modules: `lib/todo_list/task/commands/{slice_name}.ex`
- Event modules: `lib/todo_list/task/events/{event_name}.ex`
- Test files (should be per-command to avoid conflicts):
  - `test/todo_list/task/commands/{command_name}_test.exs`
  - `test/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector_test.exs` (shared but in separate worktrees)

## SDLC Workflow Per Slice

Each teammate should follow:
1. Pick up their assigned dot task (`dot on <task-id>`)
2. Run TDD cycle for each GWT scenario:
   - RED: Write ONE failing test (one assertion)
   - DOMAIN: Review for type safety, create domain types
   - GREEN: Minimal implementation to pass
   - DOMAIN: Review implementation
3. After all scenarios pass, create PR (/sdlc:pr)

## Questions to Address in the Plan

1. **Worktree creation**: How to create 7 worktrees with proper branch names
2. **Team structure**: How to create the team and assign slices
3. **Teammate prompts**: What each teammate needs to know (GWT scenarios, file paths, architecture decisions)
4. **Dot task management**: Each worktree has its own .dots/ - how to handle this
5. **PR strategy**: Should all PRs target main? Order of merging?
6. **Merge conflict resolution**: Strategy for shared files
7. **Lead role**: What the team lead does while teammates work
8. **Shutdown and cleanup**: After all PRs merged

## Key Constraints

- Agent Teams are enabled with tmux mode
- Teammates are full Claude Code sessions with access to all SDLC skills and subagents
- "No nested teams" - teammates can use subagents but not create teams
- Each teammate loads CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills automatically
- Teammates don't inherit lead's conversation history - need full context in spawn prompt
- The lead should use delegate mode (coordination only, no direct code edits)

Please design a comprehensive, step-by-step plan covering all of the above. Be specific about:
- Exact git commands for worktree creation
- Team creation parameters
- Spawn prompts for each teammate (what context they need)
- Task dependency structure
- PR merge order strategy
- How the lead monitors and coordinates

The task's 11→description is updated. 12→ 13→## Diagram 14→ 15→```mermaid 16→flowchart LR 17→ UI["Active Task List
inline edit field"] 18→ CMD["ChangeTaskDescription"]:::command 19→ EVT["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event 20→ VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view 21→ VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view 22→ 23→ UI fill:#22c55e,color:#fff 31→``` 32→ 33→## Command: ChangeTaskDescription 34→ 35→| Field | Type | Description | 36→|-------|------|-------------| 37→| task_id | identifier | The task to edit | 38→| new_description | text | The updated description (non-empty) | 39→ 40→**Triggered by:** User clicks on a task description in the active list, edits 41→the text inline, and confirms by pressing Enter data.prompt:
I need to design a detailed implementation plan for running 7 vertical slices in parallel using Claude Code Agent Teams + Git Worktrees. Here's the full context:

## Project Context

This is an event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list app using Commanded + Ecto projections. Infrastructure is complete (Commanded app, router, event store, repo all configured). No production code exists yet - only infrastructure modules.

## Architecture

- **Commanded-native aggregates** with aggregate-per-command pattern (via commanded_boilerplate)
- **Domain types** via newt library: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition
- **Context module** `TodoList.Tasks` as API boundary
- **Testing**: 3 layers - aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E
- **Quality gates**: compile (warnings-as-errors), format, dialyzer, tests

## The 7 Vertical Slices (all independent, all ready)

1. **AddTask + ActiveTaskList** (Command + View) - 3 GWT scenarios
2. **CompleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
3. **CompletedTaskList** (View) - 6 GWT scenarios
4. **ReopenTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
5. **ChangeTaskDescription** (Command) - 4 GWT scenarios
6. **ReprioritizeTask** (Command) - 3 GWT scenarios
7. **DeleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios

## GWT Scenarios Location
docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/ contains:
- add-task.md, complete-task.md, reopen-task.md, change-task-description.md
- reprioritize-task.md, delete-task.md, active-task-list.md, completed-task-list.md

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

The plan is to:
1. Create 7 git worktrees (one per slice)
2. Create an Agent Team with 7 teammates (one per slice)
3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

These files will be created/modified by ALL slices independently in their worktrees:
- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema
- `lib/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector.ex` - Event handlers
- `lib/todo_list/tasks.ex` - Context module API
- Domain types: `lib/todo_list/task_id.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_description.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_position.ex`
- Ecto migration(s) for the tasks table
- Router/LiveView routes

Each worktree will have its own copy, so no overwrites during development. Merge conflicts resolved during PR process.

## Files Unique Per Slice

- Command modules: `lib/todo_list/task/commands/{slice_name}.ex`
- Event modules: `lib/todo_list/task/events/{event_name}.ex`
- Test files (should be per-command to avoid conflicts):
  - `test/todo_list/task/commands/{command_name}_test.exs`
  - `test/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector_test.exs` (shared but in separate worktrees)

## SDLC Workflow Per Slice

Each teammate should follow:
1. Pick up their assigned dot task (`dot on <task-id>`)
2. Run TDD cycle for each GWT scenario:
   - RED: Write ONE failing test (one assertion)
   - DOMAIN: Review for type safety, create domain types
   - GREEN: Minimal implementation to pass
   - DOMAIN: Review implementation
3. After all scenarios pass, create PR (/sdlc:pr)

## Questions to Address in the Plan

1. **Worktree creation**: How to create 7 worktrees with proper branch names
2. **Team structure**: How to create the team and assign slices
3. **Teammate prompts**: What each teammate needs to know (GWT scenarios, file paths, architecture decisions)
4. **Dot task management**: Each worktree has its own .dots/ - how to handle this
5. **PR strategy**: Should all PRs target main? Order of merging?
6. **Merge conflict resolution**: Strategy for shared files
7. **Lead role**: What the team lead does while teammates work
8. **Shutdown and cleanup**: After all PRs merged

## Key Constraints

- Agent Teams are enabled with tmux mode
- Teammates are full Claude Code sessions with access to all SDLC skills and subagents
- "No nested teams" - teammates can use subagents but not create teams
- Each teammate loads CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills automatically
- Teammates don't inherit lead's conversation history - need full context in spawn prompt
- The lead should use delegate mode (coordination only, no direct code edits)

Please design a comprehensive, step-by-step plan covering all of the above. Be specific about:
- Exact git commands for worktree creation
- Team creation parameters
- Spawn prompts for each teammate (what context they need)
- Task dependency structure
- PR merge order strategy
- How the lead monitors and coordinates

1→# Slice: ReprioritizeTask 2→ 3→## Pattern 4→ 5→**Command** (State Change) 6→ 7→## Summary 8→ 9→The user drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a 10→new position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks. 11→ 12→## Diagram 13→ 14→```mermaid 15→flowchart LR 16→ UI["Active Task List
drag handle"] 17→ CMD["ReprioritizeTask"]:::command 18→ EVT["TaskReprioritized"]:::event 19→ VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view 20→ 21→ UI -->|"task_id, new_position"| CMD 22→ CMD --> EVT 23→ EVT --> VIEW 24→ 25→ classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff 26→ classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff 27→ classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff 28→``` 29→ 30→## Command: ReprioritizeTask 31→ 32→| Field | Type | Description | 33→|-------|------|-------------| 34→| task_id | identifier | The task being moved | 35→| new_position | number | The target position in the list | 36→ 37→**Triggered by:** User grabs a drag handle (::) and drops the task at a new 38→position in the active list. 39→ 40→**Rejection conditions:** 41→- Task dropped in the same position (no change). 42→ 43→## Event: TaskReprioritized 44→ 45→| Field | Type | Description | 46→|-------|------|-------------| 47→| task_id | identifier | The task that was moved | 48→| position | number | The new position in the priority-ordered list | 49→ 50→## Wireframe 51→ 52→``` 53→Before: 54→ 55→ :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] 56→ :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] 57→ :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] 58→ :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] 59→ 60→User drags "Fix the leaky faucet" to the top: 61→ 62→ :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] <-- moved here 63→ :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] 64→ :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] 65→ :: Write thank-you notes [v] [ x ] 66→``` 67→ 68→## Read Model Impact 69→ 70→**ActiveTaskList:** Task order updated. The moved task appears at its new 71→position; other tasks shift to accommodate. 72→ 73→## GWT Scenarios 74→ 75→### Scenario: Move a task to a higher priority (lower position number) 76→ 77→**Given** (prior events): 78→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } 79→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } 80→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 } 81→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-004", description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 4 } 82→ 83→**When** (command): 84→- ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 } 85→ 86→**Then** (events produced): 87→- TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 } 88→ 89→--- 90→ 91→### Scenario: Move a task to a lower priority (higher position number) 92→ 93→**Given** (prior events): 94→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } 95→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } 96→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 } 97→ 98→**When** (command): 99→- ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 } 100→ 101→**Then** (events produced): 102→- TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 } 103→ 104→--- 105→ 106→### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change) 107→ 108→**Given** (prior events): 109→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 } 110→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 } 111→ 112→**When** (command): 113→- ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 } 114→ 115→**Then** (error -- no events): 116→- Error: "Task is already at this position" 117→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→# Slice: DeleteTask 2→ 3→## Pattern 4→ 5→**Command** (State Change) 6→ 7→## Summary 8→ 9→The user clicks the delete button on a task (from either the active list or the 10→completed tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If the user confirms, the 11→task is permanently removed from all views. 12→ 13→## Diagram 14→ 15→```mermaid 16→flowchart LR 17→ UI1["Active Task List
delete button"] 18→ UI2["Completed Tasks View
delete button"] 19→ DIALOG["Confirmation Dialog"] 20→ CMD["DeleteTask"]:::command 21→ EVT["TaskDeleted"]:::event 22→ VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view 23→ VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view 24→ 25→ UI1 -->|"click x"| DIALOG 26→ UI2 -->|"click x"| DIALOG 27→ DIALOG -->|"confirm, task_id"| CMD 28→ CMD --> EVT 29→ EVT --> VIEW1 30→ EVT --> VIEW2 31→ 32→ classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff 33→ classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff 34→ classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff 35→``` 36→ 37→## Command: DeleteTask 38→ 39→| Field | Type | Description | 40→|-------|------|-------------| 41→| task_id | identifier | The task to delete | 42→ 43→**Triggered by:** User clicks [x] on any task (active or completed), then 44→clicks [Delete] in the confirmation dialog. 45→ 46→**Rejection conditions:** 47→- User clicks [Cancel] in the confirmation dialog (command is never issued). 48→ 49→## Event: TaskDeleted 50→ 51→| Field | Type | Description | 52→|-------|------|-------------| 53→| task_id | identifier | The task that was deleted | 54→ 55→**Business rule:** Deletion is permanent from the UI perspective. data.prompt:
I need to design a detailed implementation plan for running 7 vertical slices in parallel using Claude Code Agent Teams + Git Worktrees. Here's the full context:

## Project Context

This is an event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list app using Commanded + Ecto projections. Infrastructure is complete (Commanded app, router, event store, repo all configured). No production code exists yet - only infrastructure modules.

## Architecture

- **Commanded-native aggregates** with aggregate-per-command pattern (via commanded_boilerplate)
- **Domain types** via newt library: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition
- **Context module** `TodoList.Tasks` as API boundary
- **Testing**: 3 layers - aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E
- **Quality gates**: compile (warnings-as-errors), format, dialyzer, tests

## The 7 Vertical Slices (all independent, all ready)

1. **AddTask + ActiveTaskList** (Command + View) - 3 GWT scenarios
2. **CompleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
3. **CompletedTaskList** (View) - 6 GWT scenarios
4. **ReopenTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
5. **ChangeTaskDescription** (Command) - 4 GWT scenarios
6. **ReprioritizeTask** (Command) - 3 GWT scenarios
7. **DeleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios

## GWT Scenarios Location
docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/ contains:
- add-task.md, complete-task.md, reopen-task.md, change-task-description.md
- reprioritize-task.md, delete-task.md, active-task-list.md, completed-task-list.md

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

The plan is to:
1. Create 7 git worktrees (one per slice)
2. Create an Agent Team with 7 teammates (one per slice)
3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

These files will be created/modified by ALL slices independently in their worktrees:
- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema
- `lib/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector.ex` - Event handlers
- `lib/todo_list/tasks.ex` - Context module API
- Domain types: `lib/todo_list/task_id.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_description.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_position.ex`
- Ecto migration(s) for the tasks table
- Router/LiveView routes

Each worktree will have its own copy, so no overwrites during development. Merge conflicts resolved during PR process.

## Files Unique Per Slice

- Command modules: `lib/todo_list/task/commands/{slice_name}.ex`
- Event modules: `lib/todo_list/task/events/{event_name}.ex`
- Test files (should be per-command to avoid conflicts):
  - `test/todo_list/task/commands/{command_name}_test.exs`
  - `test/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector_test.exs` (shared but in separate worktrees)

## SDLC Workflow Per Slice

Each teammate should follow:
1. Pick up their assigned dot task (`dot on <task-id>`)
2. Run TDD cycle for each GWT scenario:
   - RED: Write ONE failing test (one assertion)
   - DOMAIN: Review for type safety, create domain types
   - GREEN: Minimal implementation to pass
   - DOMAIN: Review implementation
3. After all scenarios pass, create PR (/sdlc:pr)

## Questions to Address in the Plan

1. **Worktree creation**: How to create 7 worktrees with proper branch names
2. **Team structure**: How to create the team and assign slices
3. **Teammate prompts**: What each teammate needs to know (GWT scenarios, file paths, architecture decisions)
4. **Dot task management**: Each worktree has its own .dots/ - how to handle this
5. **PR strategy**: Should all PRs target main? Order of merging?
6. **Merge conflict resolution**: Strategy for shared files
7. **Lead role**: What the team lead does while teammates work
8. **Shutdown and cleanup**: After all PRs merged

## Key Constraints

- Agent Teams are enabled with tmux mode
- Teammates are full Claude Code sessions with access to all SDLC skills and subagents
- "No nested teams" - teammates can use subagents but not create teams
- Each teammate loads CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills automatically
- Teammates don't inherit lead's conversation history - need full context in spawn prompt
- The lead should use delegate mode (coordination only, no direct code edits)

Please design a comprehensive, step-by-step plan covering all of the above. Be specific about:
- Exact git commands for worktree creation
- Team creation parameters
- Spawn prompts for each teammate (what context they need)
- Task dependency structure
- PR merge order strategy
- How the lead monitors and coordinates

content.content:
1→defmodule TodoList.Application do
     2→  # See https://hexdocs.pm/elixir/Application.html
     3→  # for more information on OTP Applications
     4→  @moduledoc false
     5→
     6→  use Application
     7→
     8→  @impl true
     9→  def start(_type, _args) do
    10→    children = [
    11→      TodoListWeb.Telemetry,
    12→      TodoList.Repo,
    13→      {DNSCluster, query: Application.get_env(:todo_list, :dns_cluster_query) || :ignore},
    14→      {Phoenix.PubSub, name: TodoList.PubSub},
    15→      TodoList.App,
    16→      # Start to serve requests, typically the last entry
    17→      TodoListWeb.Endpoint
    18→    ]
    19→
    20→    # See https://hexdocs.pm/elixir/Supervisor.html
    21→    # for other strategies and supported options
    22→    opts = [strategy: :one_for_one, name: TodoList.Supervisor]
    23→    Supervisor.start_link(children, opts)
    24→  end
    25→
    26→  # Tell Phoenix to update the endpoint configuration
    27→  # whenever the application is updated.
    28→  @impl true
    29→  def config_change(changed, _new, removed) do
    30→    TodoListWeb.Endpoint.config_change(changed, removed)
    31→    :ok
    32→  end
    33→end
    34→

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

The plan is to:
1. Create 7 git worktrees (one per slice)
2. Create an Agent Team with 7 teammates (one per slice)
3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

These files will be created/modified by ALL slices independently in their worktrees:
- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema
- `lib/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector.ex` - Event handlers
- `lib/todo_list/tasks.ex` - Context module API
- Domain types: `lib/todo_list/task_id.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_description.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_position.ex`
- Ecto migration(s) for the tasks table
- Router/LiveView routes

Each worktree will have its own copy, so no overwrites during development. Merge conflicts resolved during PR process.

## Files Unique Per Slice

- Command modules: `lib/todo_list/task/commands/{slice_name}.ex`
- Event modules: `lib/todo_list/task/events/{event_name}.ex`
- Test files (should be per-command to avoid conflicts):
  - `test/todo_list/task/commands/{command_name}_test.exs`
  - `test/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector_test.exs` (shared but in separate worktrees)

## SDLC Workflow Per Slice

Each teammate should follow:
1. Pick up their assigned dot task (`dot on <task-id>`)
2. Run TDD cycle for each GWT scenario:
   - RED: Write ONE failing test (one assertion)
   - DOMAIN: Review for type safety, create domain types
   - GREEN: Minimal implementation to pass
   - DOMAIN: Review implementation
3. After all scenarios pass, create PR (/sdlc:pr)

## Questions to Address in the Plan

1. **Worktree creation**: How to create 7 worktrees with proper branch names
2. **Team structure**: How to create the team and assign slices
3. **Teammate prompts**: What each teammate needs to know (GWT scenarios, file paths, architecture decisions)
4. **Dot task management**: Each worktree has its own .dots/ - how to handle this
5. **PR strategy**: Should all PRs target main? Order of merging?
6. **Merge conflict resolution**: Strategy for shared files
7. **Lead role**: What the team lead does while teammates work
8. **Shutdown and cleanup**: After all PRs merged

## Key Constraints

- Agent Teams are enabled with tmux mode
- Teammates are full Claude Code sessions with access to all SDLC skills and subagents
- "No nested teams" - teammates can use subagents but not create teams
- Each teammate loads CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills automatically
- Teammates don't inherit lead's conversation history - need full context in spawn prompt
- The lead should use delegate mode (coordination only, no direct code edits)

Please design a comprehensive, step-by-step plan covering all of the above. Be specific about:
- Exact git commands for worktree creation
- Team creation parameters
- Spawn prompts for each teammate (what context they need)
- Task dependency structure
- PR merge order strategy
- How the lead monitors and coordinates

1→import Config
     2→
     3→# Configure your database
     4→#
     5→# The MIX_TEST_PARTITION environment variable can be used
     6→# to provide built-in test partitioning in CI environment.
     7→# Run `mix help test` for more information.
     8→config :todo_list, TodoList.Repo,
     9→  username: "postgres",
    10→  password: "postgres",
    11→  hostname: "localhost",
    12→  database: "todo_list_test#{System.get_env("MIX_TEST_PARTITION")}",
    13→  pool: Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox,
    14→  pool_size: System.schedulers_online() * 2
    15→
    16→# We don't run a server during test. If one is required,
    17→# you can enable the server option below.
    18→config :todo_list, TodoListWeb.Endpoint,
    19→  http: [ip: {127, 0, 0, 1}, port: 4002],
    20→  secret_key_base: "UZOdotzn/Z5FwbHhrPEB5VMaqE22rKeJfstXaQzglLZzBoHhCO9VIRSZ2NIGpvyT",
    21→  server: false
    22→
    23→# In test we don't send emails
    24→config :todo_list, TodoList.Mailer, adapter: Swoosh.Adapters.Test
    25→
    26→# Disable swoosh api client as it is only required for production adapters
    27→config :swoosh, :api_client, false
    28→
    29→# Print only warnings and errors during test
    30→config :logger, level: :warning
    31→
    32→# Initialize plugs at runtime for faster test compilation
    33→config :phoenix, :plug_init_mode, :runtime
    34→
    35→# Enable helpful, but potentially expensive runtime checks
    36→config :phoenix_live_view,
    37→  enable_expensive_runtime_checks: true
    38→
    39→# Sort query params output of verified routes for robust url comparisons
    40→config :phoenix,
    41→  sort_verified_routes_query_params: true
    42→
    43→# Use in-memory event store for fast, isolated tests
    44→config :todo_list, TodoList.App,
    45→  event_store: [
    46→    adapter: Commanded.EventStore.Adapters.InMemory,
    47→    serializer: Commanded.Serialization.JsonSerializer
    48→  ]
    49→

1→defmodule TodoList.MixProject do
     2→  use Mix.Project
     3→
     4→  def project do
     5→    [
     6→      app: :todo_list,
     7→      version: "0.1.0",
     8→      elixir: "~> 1.15",
     9→      elixirc_paths: elixirc_paths(Mix.env()),
    10→      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
    11→      aliases: aliases(),
    12→      deps: deps(),
    13→      compilers: [:phoenix_live_view] ++ Mix.compilers(),
    14→      listeners: [Phoenix.CodeReloader],
    15→      dialyzer: [plt_add_apps: [:ex_unit]],
    16→      event_stores: [TodoList.EventStore]
    17→    ]
    18→  end
    19→
    20→  # Configuration for the OTP application.
    21→  #
    22→  # Type `mix help compile.app` for more information.
    23→  def application do
    24→    [
    25→      mod: {TodoList.Application, []},
    26→      extra_applications: [:logger, :runtime_tools]
    27→    ]
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  def cli do
    31→    [
    32→      preferred_envs: [precommit: :test]
    33→    ]
    34→  end
    35→
    36→  # Specifies which paths to compile per environment.
    37→  defp elixirc_paths(:test), do: ["lib", "test/support"]
    38→  defp elixirc_paths(_), do: ["lib"]
    39→
    40→  # Specifies your project dependencies.
    41→  #
    42→  # Type `mix help deps` for examples and options.
    43→  defp deps do
    44→    [
    45→      {:phoenix, "~> 1.8.3"},
    46→      {:phoenix_ecto, "~> 4.5"},
    47→      {:ecto_sql, "~> 3.13"},
    48→      {:postgrex, ">= 0.0.0"},
    49→      {:phoenix_html, "~> 4.1"},
    50→      {:phoenix_live_reload, "~> 1.2", only: :dev},
    51→      {:phoenix_live_view, "~> 1.1.0"},
    52→      {:lazy_html, ">= 0.1.0", only: :test},
    53→      {:phoenix_live_dashboard, "~> 0.8.3"},
    54→      {:esbuild, "~> 0.10", runtime: Mix.env() == :dev},
    55→      {:tailwind, "~> 0.3", runtime: Mix.env() == :dev},
    56→      {:heroicons,
    57→       github: "tailwindlabs/heroicons",
    58→       tag: "v2.2.0",
    59→       sparse: "optimized",
    60→       app: false,
    61→       compile: false,
    62→       depth: 1},
    63→      {:swoosh, "~> 1.16"},
    64→      {:req, "~> 0.5"},
    65→      {:telemetry_metrics, "~> 1.0"},
    66→      {:telemetry_poller, "~> 1.0"},
    67→      {:gettext, "~> 1.0"},
    68→      {:jason, "~> 1.2"},
    69→      {:dns_cluster, "~> 0.2.0"},
    70→      {:bandit, "~> 1.5"},
    71→      {:phoenix_storybook, "~> 0.9.0", only: [:dev, :test]},
    72→      {:dialyxir, "~> 1.4", only: [:dev, :test], runtime: false},
    73→      {:commanded, "~> 1.4"},
    74→      {:commanded_eventstore_adapter, "~> 1.4"},
    75→      {:commanded_ecto_projections, "~> 1.3"},
    76→      {:eventstore, "~> 1.4"},
    77→      {:commanded_boilerplate, github: "jwilger/commanded_boilerplate"},
    78→      {:uuidv7, "~> 1.0"}
    79→    ]
    80→  end
    81→
    82→  # Aliases are shortcuts or tasks specific to the current project.
    83→  # For example, to install project dependencies and perform other setup tasks, run:
    84→  #
    85→  #     $ mix setup
    86→  #
    87→  # See the documentation for `Mix` for more info on aliases.
    88→  defp aliases do
    89→    [
    90→      setup: ["deps.get", "ecto.setup", "assets.setup", "assets.build"],
    91→      "ecto.setup": ["ecto.create", "ecto.migrate", "run priv/repo/seeds.exs"],
    92→      "ecto.reset": ["ecto.drop", "ecto.setup"],
    93→      test: ["ecto.create --quiet", "ecto.migrate --quiet", "test"],
    94→      "assets.setup": ["tailwind.install --if-missing", "esbuild.install --if-missing"],
    95→      "assets.build": [
    96→        "compile",
    97→        "tailwind todo_list",
    98→        "esbuild todo_list",
    99→        "tailwind storybook",
   100→        "esbuild storybook"
   101→      ],
   102→      "assets.deploy": [
   103→        "tailwind todo_list --minify",
   104→        "esbuild todo_list --minify",
   105→        "phx.digest"
   106→      ],
   107→      precommit: [
   108→        "deps.unlock --unused",
   109→        "compile --warnings-as-errors",
   110→        "format --check-formatted",
   111→        "dialyzer",
   112→        "test"
   113→      ]
   114→    ]
   115→  end
   116→end
   117→

I need to design a detailed implementation plan for running 7 vertical slices in parallel using Claude Code Agent Teams + Git Worktrees. Here's the full context:

## Project Context

This is an event-sourced Phoenix/LiveView todo list app using Commanded + Ecto projections. Infrastructure is complete (Commanded app, router, event store, repo all configured). No production code exists yet - only infrastructure modules.

## Architecture

- **Commanded-native aggregates** with aggregate-per-command pattern (via commanded_boilerplate)
- **Domain types** via newt library: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition
- **Context module** `TodoList.Tasks` as API boundary
- **Testing**: 3 layers - aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E
- **Quality gates**: compile (warnings-as-errors), format, dialyzer, tests

## The 7 Vertical Slices (all independent, all ready)

1. **AddTask + ActiveTaskList** (Command + View) - 3 GWT scenarios
2. **CompleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
3. **CompletedTaskList** (View) - 6 GWT scenarios
4. **ReopenTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios
5. **ChangeTaskDescription** (Command) - 4 GWT scenarios
6. **ReprioritizeTask** (Command) - 3 GWT scenarios
7. **DeleteTask** (Command) - 2 GWT scenarios

## GWT Scenarios Location
docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/ contains:
- add-task.md, complete-task.md, reopen-task.md, change-task-description.md
- reprioritize-task.md, delete-task.md, active-task-list.md, completed-task-list.md

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

The plan is to:
1. Create 7 git worktrees (one per slice)
2. Create an Agent Team with 7 teammates (one per slice)
3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

These files will be created/modified by ALL slices independently in their worktrees:
- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema
- `lib/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector.ex` - Event handlers
- `lib/todo_list/tasks.ex` - Context module API
- Domain types: `lib/todo_list/task_id.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_description.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_position.ex`
- Ecto migration(s) for the tasks table
- Router/LiveView routes

Each worktree will have its own copy, so no overwrites during development. Merge conflicts resolved during PR process.

## Files Unique Per Slice

- Command modules: `lib/todo_list/task/commands/{slice_name}.ex`
- Event modules: `lib/todo_list/task/events/{event_name}.ex`
- Test files (should be per-command to avoid conflicts):
  - `test/todo_list/task/commands/{command_name}_test.exs`
  - `test/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector_test.exs` (shared but in separate worktrees)

## SDLC Workflow Per Slice

Each teammate should follow:
1. Pick up their assigned dot task (`dot on <task-id>`)
2. Run TDD cycle for each GWT scenario:
   - RED: Write ONE failing test (one assertion)
   - DOMAIN: Review for type safety, create domain types
   - GREEN: Minimal implementation to pass
   - DOMAIN: Review implementation
3. After all scenarios pass, create PR (/sdlc:pr)

## Questions to Address in the Plan

1. **Worktree creation**: How to create 7 worktrees with proper branch names
2. **Team structure**: How to create the team and assign slices
3. **Teammate prompts**: What each teammate needs to know (GWT scenarios, file paths, architecture decisions)
4. **Dot task management**: Each worktree has its own .dots/ - how to handle this
5. **PR strategy**: Should all PRs target main? Order of merging?
6. **Merge conflict resolution**: Strategy for shared files
7. **Lead role**: What the team lead does while teammates work
8. **Shutdown and cleanup**: After all PRs merged

## Key Constraints

- Agent Teams are enabled with tmux mode
- Teammates are full Claude Code sessions with access to all SDLC skills and subagents
- "No nested teams" - teammates can use subagents but not create teams
- Each teammate loads CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills automatically
- Teammates don't inherit lead's conversation history - need full context in spawn prompt
- The lead should use delegate mode (coordination only, no direct code edits)

Please design a comprehensive, step-by-step plan covering all of the above. Be specific about:
- Exact git commands for worktree creation
- Team creation parameters
- Spawn prompts for each teammate (what context they need)
- Task dependency structure
- PR merge order strategy
- How the lead monitors and coordinates

content.content:
1→defmodule TodoListWeb.Router do
     2→  use TodoListWeb, :router
     3→
     4→  pipeline :browser do
     5→    plug :accepts, ["html"]
     6→    plug :fetch_session
     7→    plug :fetch_live_flash
     8→    plug :put_root_layout, html: {TodoListWeb.Layouts, :root}
     9→    plug :protect_from_forgery
    10→    plug :put_secure_browser_headers
    11→  end
    12→
    13→  pipeline :api do
    14→    plug :accepts, ["json"]
    15→  end
    16→
    17→  scope "/", TodoListWeb do
    18→    pipe_through :browser
    19→
    20→    get "/", PageController, :home
    21→  end
    22→
    23→  # Other scopes may use custom stacks.
    24→  # scope "/api", TodoListWeb do
    25→  #   pipe_through :api
    26→  # end
    27→
    28→  # Enable LiveDashboard, Swoosh mailbox, and Storybook in development
    29→  if Application.compile_env(:todo_list, :dev_routes) do
    30→    import Phoenix.LiveDashboard.Router
    31→    import PhoenixStorybook.Router
    32→
    33→    scope "/" do
    34→      storybook_assets()
    35→    end
    36→
    37→    scope "/dev" do
    38→      pipe_through :browser
    39→
    40→      live_dashboard "/dashboard", metrics: TodoListWeb.Telemetry
    41→      forward "/mailbox", Plug.Swoosh.MailboxPreview
    42→    end
    43→
    44→    scope "/" do
    45→      pipe_through :browser
    46→      live_storybook("/dev/storybook", backend_module: TodoListWeb.Storybook)
    47→    end
    48→  end
    49→end
    50→

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema

content.content:
1→---
     2→title: AddTask + ActiveTaskList
     3→status: open
     4→priority: 2
     5→issue-type: task
     6→created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:23.653757-08:00"
     7→blocks:
     8→  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Slices: AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View)
    12→
    13→**Pattern**: Command + View (implemented together -- can't test Add without a View)
    14→
    15→### AddTask Command
    16→User types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the
    17→bottom of the active list with the given description and a default position.
    18→
    19→**Command fields:** description (text, non-empty)
    20→**Event:** TaskAdded { task_id, description, position }
    21→**Rejection:** Description is empty or blank (prevented by type system)
    22→
    23→### ActiveTaskList View
    24→Read model projecting all events into an ordered list of active (incomplete,
    25→non-deleted) tasks. Powers the main view.
    26→
    27→**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description, position }, completed_count
    28→**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
    29→**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)
    30→
    31→### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command
    32→
    33→#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
    34→- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
    35→- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
    36→- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
    37→
    38→#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom)
    39→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
    40→- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
    41→- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }
    42→
    43→#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed (position based on count)
    44→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, position: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
    45→- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
    46→- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 }
    47→
    48→### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View
    49→
    50→#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of empty list
    51→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
    52→- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
    53→- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }
    54→
    55→#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of populated list
    56→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks
    57→- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
    58→- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at bottom
    59→
    60→#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count
    61→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
    62→- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
    63→- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 2
    64→
    65→#### Scenario: TaskReopened adds task to top and decrements count
    66→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
    67→- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
    68→- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened at position 1, others shift down, completed_count: 1
    69→
    70→#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates active task description
    71→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
    72→- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
    73→- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"
    74→
    75→#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for completed task -- no effect on active list
    76→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
    77→- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
    78→- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list
    79→
    80→#### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to new position
    81→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks
    82→- [ ] **When**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
    83→- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down
    84→
    85→#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes active task
    86→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
    87→- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
    88→- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1
    89→
    90→#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for completed task decrements count
    91→- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 1 task, completed_count: 2
    92→- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" } (completed task)
    93→- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1
    94→
    95→### Implementation Notes
    96→- Three test layers: aggregate unit tests, projector integration tests, LiveView E2E tests
    97→- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
    98→- TaskProjector handles projection to Ecto schema
    99→- TodoList.Tasks context as API boundary
   100→- Domain types: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition (via newt)
   101→
   102→---
   103→Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/add-task.md, active-task-list.md
   104→
   105→

1→---
     2→title: CompletedTaskList
     3→status: open
     4→priority: 2
     5→issue-type: task
     6→created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:45.442732-08:00"
     7→blocks:
     8→  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Slice: CompletedTaskList
    12→
    13→**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)
    14→
    15→### Description
    16→Read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
    17→tasks. Powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
    18→and reopen or delete tasks.
    19→
    20→**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description }
    21→**Filter:** completed = true AND deleted = false
    22→**Order:** By completion time descending (most recently completed first)
    23→
    24→### Acceptance Criteria
    25→
    26→#### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
    27→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
    28→- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
    29→- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
    30→
    31→#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
    32→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
    33→- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
    34→- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }
    35→
    36→#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
    37→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
    38→- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
    39→- [ ] **Then**: task-002 at top, task-001 below (most recent first)
    40→
    41→#### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list
    42→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
    43→- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
    44→- [ ] **Then**: Only task-001 remains
    45→
    46→#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates completed task description
    47→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001 "Buy groceries"
    48→- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }
    49→- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Buy groceries and snacks"
    50→
    51→#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect
    52→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
    53→- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
    54→- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list
    55→
    56→#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes completed task
    57→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
    58→- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }
    59→- [ ] **Then**: Only task-002 remains
    60→
    61→#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for active task -- no effect on completed list
    62→- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
    63→- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
    64→- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list
    65→
    66→### Implementation Notes
    67→- Same TaskProjector handles both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList projections
    68→- Projection schema tracks completed status and completion timestamp
    69→- TodoList.Tasks.completed_tasks() query function returns completed tasks ordered by completion_time desc
    70→- Completed tasks LiveView at /tasks/completed
    71→
    72→---
    73→Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/completed-task-list.md
    74→
    75→

1→---
     2→title: ReopenTask
     3→status: open
     4→priority: 2
     5→issue-type: task
     6→created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:54.067118-08:00"
     7→blocks:
     8→  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Slice: ReopenTask
    12→
    13→**Pattern**: Command (State Change)
    14→
    15→### Description
    16→User clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete
    17→and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position).
    18→
    19→**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
    20→**Event:** TaskReopened { task_id }
    21→**Business rule:** Reopened task placed at top (highest priority) -- rationale: likely urgent if user reopens it
    22→**Rejection:** Task is not completed (already active)
    23→
    24→### Acceptance Criteria
    25→
    26→#### Scenario: Reopen a completed task (goes to top of active list)
    27→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-002 }
    28→- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" }
    29→- [ ] **Then**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
    30→
    31→#### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active
    32→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
    33→- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" }
    34→- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotCompleted
    35→
    36→### Implementation Notes
    37→- Aggregate needs: task_id, completed (boolean)
    38→- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskNotCompleted (via newt)
    39→- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList adds to top (position 1, shift others), CompletedTaskList removes task
    40→

- reprioritize-task.md, delete-task.md, active-task-list.md, completed-task-list.md

## Approach: Agent Teams + Git Worktrees

The plan is to:
1. Create 7 git worktrees (one per slice)
2. Create an Agent Team with 7 teammates (one per slice)
3. Each teammate works in its own worktree on its own feature branch
4. Each teammate runs the full SDLC TDD cycle (/sdlc:work or equivalent) for its slice
5. Each teammate creates a PR when done
6. Merge conflicts in shared files handled at PR merge time

## Shared Files Across Slices

These files will be created/modified by ALL slices independently in their worktrees:
- `lib/todo_list/task/projections/task_projection.ex` - Ecto schema
- `lib/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector.ex` - Event handlers
- `lib/todo_list/tasks.ex` - Context module API
- Domain types: `lib/todo_list/task_id.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_description.ex`, `lib/todo_list/task_position.ex`
- Ecto migration(s) for the tasks table
- Router/LiveView routes

Each worktree will have its own copy, so no overwrites during development. Merge conflicts resolved during PR process.

## Files Unique Per Slice

- Command modules: `lib/todo_list/task/commands/{slice_name}.ex`
- Event modules: `lib/todo_list/task/events/{event_name}.ex`
- Test files (should be per-command to avoid conflicts):
  - `test/todo_list/task/commands/{command_name}_test.exs`
  - `test/todo_list/task/projectors/task_projector_test.exs` (shared but in separate worktrees)

## SDLC Workflow Per Slice

Each teammate should follow:
1. Pick up their assigned dot task (`dot on <task-id>`)
2. Run TDD cycle for each GWT scenario:
   - RED: Write ONE failing test (one assertion)
   - DOMAIN: Review for type safety, create domain types
   - GREEN: Minimal implementation to pass
   - DOMAIN: Review implementation
3. After all scenarios pass, create PR (/sdlc:pr)

## Questions to Address in the Plan

1. **Worktree creation**: How to create 7 worktrees with proper branch names
2. **Team structure**: How to create the team and assign slices
3. **Teammate prompts**: What each teammate needs to know (GWT scenarios, file paths, architecture decisions)
4. **Dot task management**: Each worktree has its own .dots/ - how to handle this
5. **PR strategy**: Should all PRs target main? Order of merging?
6. **Merge conflict resolution**: Strategy for shared files
7. **Lead role**: What the team lead does while teammates work
8. **Shutdown and cleanup**: After all PRs merged

## Key Constraints

- Agent Teams are enabled with tmux mode
- Teammates are full Claude Code sessions with access to all SDLC skills and subagents
- "No nested teams" - teammates can use subagents but not create teams
- Each teammate loads CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, and skills automatically
- Teammates don't inherit lead's conversation history - need full context in spawn prompt
- The lead should use delegate mode (coordination only, no direct code edits)

Please design a comprehensive, step-by-step plan covering all of the above. Be specific about:
- Exact git commands for worktree creation
- Team creation parameters
- Spawn prompts for each teammate (what context they need)
- Task dependency structure
- PR merge order strategy
- How the lead monitors and coordinates

1→---
     2→title: ChangeTaskDescription
     3→status: open
     4→priority: 2
     5→issue-type: task
     6→created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:05.416279-08:00"
     7→blocks:
     8→  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Slice: ChangeTaskDescription
    12→
    13→**Pattern**: Command (State Change)
    14→
    15→### Description
    16→User clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes
    17→the text, and confirms (Enter or blur). The task's description is updated.
    18→
    19→**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_description (text, non-empty)
    20→**Event:** TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id, description }
    21→**Rejection:** Empty/blank description (type system) OR identical to current (no change)
    22→
    23→### Acceptance Criteria
    24→
    25→#### Scenario: Change a task's description
    26→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
    27→- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
    28→- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
    29→
    30→#### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change)
    31→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
    32→- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" }
    33→- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged
    34→
    35→#### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed
    36→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
    37→- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
    38→- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
    39→
    40→#### Scenario: Reject changing to current value after prior change
    41→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
    42→- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
    43→- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged
    44→
    45→### Implementation Notes
    46→- Aggregate needs: task_id, description (current value)
    47→- Domain error type: TodoList.DescriptionUnchanged (via newt)
    48→- Empty/blank validation handled by TaskDescription newt type, not command rejection
    49→- Read model impact: both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList update description
    50→
    51→---
    52→Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/change-task-description.md
    53→
    54→

1→---
     2→title: ReprioritizeTask
     3→status: open
     4→priority: 2
     5→issue-type: task
     6→created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:14.983549-08:00"
     7→blocks:
     8→  - todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Slice: ReprioritizeTask
    12→
    13→**Pattern**: Command (State Change)
    14→
    15→### Description
    16→User drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new
    17→position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks.
    18→
    19→**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_position (number)
    20→**Event:** TaskReprioritized { task_id, position }
    21→**Rejection:** Task dropped in same position (no change)
    22→
    23→### Acceptance Criteria
    24→
    25→#### Scenario: Move task to higher priority (lower position number)
    26→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }, TaskAdded { task-004, pos: 4 }
    27→- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 }
    28→- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
    29→
    30→#### Scenario: Move task to lower priority (higher position number)
    31→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }
    32→- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 }
    33→- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }
    34→
    35→#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change)
    36→- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }
    37→- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 }
    38→- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyAtPosition
    39→
    40→### Implementation Notes
    41→- Aggregate needs: task_id, position (current value)
    42→- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyAtPosition (via newt)
    43→- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList shifts positions of surrounding tasks
    44→- LiveView triggers via drag-and-drop (phx-hook for sortable)
    45→
    46→---
    47→Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reprioritize-task.md
    48→
